human_readable_summary: This ledger records what backprop-trace's V8/Node 22 engine (src/general-engine.ts) produces for one SGD step on a 4-3-3-sigmoid topology applied to the canonical first iris flower (Fisher 1936 sample row 1: sepal_length=5.1, sepal_width=3.5, petal_length=1.4, petal_width=0.2; target one-hot [1, 0, 0] for Iris setosa). Initial weights are operator-chosen deterministic monotonically-increasing values (0.01, 0.02, ..., 0.23). No published step-1 trace exists in the literature for the iris dataset under this exact topology/init/optimizer combination — the trace is engine-anchored.
engine_reproduction_source: fixtures/iris.golden.jsonl is the engine-emitted first-run output of src/general-engine.ts on IRIS_INPUT (V8/Node 22).

source.anchor: engine-anchored (no published step-1 trace exists for iris 4-3-3-sigmoid with these specific initial weights)

dataset_reference.author: R. A. Fisher
dataset_reference.title: The Use of Multiple Measurements in Taxonomic Problems
dataset_reference.summary: The iris dataset (150 flowers, 4 features, 3 species) is one of the most-cited datasets in statistical ML pedagogy. This fixture uses the first sample row (Iris setosa) only — a single training example chosen for canonicity and reproducibility, NOT a full training run.

operator_chosen_init.rationale: Deterministic monotonically-increasing weights stepping by 0.01 in topology.parameter_order. 12 input-to-hidden weights take values 0.01..0.12, 9 hidden-to-output weights take values 0.13..0.21, hidden bias is 0.22, output bias is 0.23. This pattern makes the trace reproducible byte-for-byte and the order of parameters in parameter_order visible from the parameters_before values alone.
operator_chosen_init.parameter_layout: 12 input-to-hidden (w_f1_h1..w_f4_h3 in order: h1 first then h2 then h3, each across f1..f4) + 9 hidden-to-output (w_h1_y1..w_h3_y3 in order: y1 first then y2 then y3, each across h1..h3) + b_hidden + b_output

sample.interpretation: First iris flower in Fisher's 1936 dataset (Iris setosa)

hyperparameters.bias_policy: constant (per v0.3 design; bias updates deferred to v0.4)

claim_type_legend.source_published_input: Constant input or hyperparameter explicitly given in the source
claim_type_legend.source_published_loss: Loss value explicitly given in the source
claim_type_legend.source_published_forward: Forward-pass value explicitly given in the source
claim_type_legend.source_published_update: Post-update parameter value explicitly given in the source
claim_type_legend.widely_cited_downstream_anchor: Value commonly repeated in downstream pedagogy; not given an explicit derivation in the source at this precision
claim_type_legend.engine_anchored: No published source provides this value; the engine's first-run output is the canonical anchor

reproduction_status_legend.verified: Engine reproduces this value within tolerance
reproduction_status_legend.drift_observed: Engine produces a different value; documented in drift_note. Hard-gate value (true/false) governs whether drift fails the build or merely WARNs.

claims.provenance: Sum of half squared errors across all 3 outputs (y1, y2, y3) on the pre-update forward pass. Setosa target [1,0,0] vs engine forward [0.627049474, 0.641357562, 0.655418375]. Per-output: y1=0.0695460473 (target 1, output 0.627 is below), y2=0.205669761 (target 0, output 0.641 is well above), y3=0.214786623 (target 0, output 0.655 is well above).

Post-update total error after one SGD step from the operator-chosen init. Decrease vs pre-update loss_total demonstrates the step moved in the descent direction (0.490002431 -> 0.481817288, delta -0.008185143). Engine value pinned at 9 significant digits per numeric_policy.precision_significant_digits.
claims.v0_3_behavior: Engine-anchored: any drift here would mean the engine math or canonical emission changed. bp verify general fails (hard gate) on drift.

claims.provenance: sigmoid(net_y1) for the setosa output. net_y1 = w_h1_y1*out_h1 + w_h2_y1*out_h2 + w_h3_y1*out_h3 + b_output.

Output error signal for y1 (setosa): (1 - out_y1) * sigmoid'(net_y1) = positive (engine should push y1 UP toward the target of 1). Confirms descent-direction sign convention is consistent across multi-output topologies.

Post-update forward pass through the same network with parameters_after. Confirms the SGD step pushed the setosa output closer to target=1 (0.627049474 -> 0.629175379, delta +0.002125905). The other two outputs (y2, y3) are pushed DOWN toward target=0.

standards_referenced: Pineau et al. JMLR 2021 — ML Reproducibility Checklist (https://arxiv.org/abs/2003.12206)
Mitchell et al. FAT* 2019 — Model Cards (https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.03993)
Gebru et al. CACM 2021 — Datasheets for Datasets (https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.09010)
Kapoor et al. Science Advances 2024 — REFORMS (https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/sciadv.adk3452)
Jia et al. IEEE S&P 2021 — Proof-of-Learning (https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2103.05633)
Fisher 1936 — The Use of Multiple Measurements in Taxonomic Problems (https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/datasets/iris)

caveats_and_recommendations.scope: single-step iris (one sample, one SGD step) with operator-chosen deterministic init (v0.3 SCOPE)
caveats_and_recommendations.activation_hidden: sigmoid (Math.exp via V8 fdlibm port; deterministic on Node 22 V8)
caveats_and_recommendations.activation_output: sigmoid (NOT softmax — softmax deferred to v0.4)
caveats_and_recommendations.batching: none (single sample = the first row of Fisher's iris dataset)
caveats_and_recommendations.optimizer: sgd (no momentum, no Adam, no weight decay)
caveats_and_recommendations.bias_policy: constant in step 1 (per v0.3 design; bias updates deferred to v0.4)
caveats_and_recommendations.cross_engine_portability: V8 only; Hermes / JSC / Bun-JSC untested
caveats_and_recommendations.cross_node_major_portability: tested on Node 22.x only; Math.exp is not contractually bit-stable across V8 majors
caveats_and_recommendations.training_scope: ONE sample, ONE SGD step. NOT a converged classifier — convergence to ~95-99% setosa accuracy requires many epochs across all 150 samples, which is outside v0.3 scope. v0.3 ships only the canonical first-step trace from the operator-chosen init.
caveats_and_recommendations.loss_choice_note: Sigmoid + MSE is suboptimal for multi-class classification (cross-entropy + softmax converges faster). This fixture uses MSE because v0.3 only ships half_squared_error as a loss option; cross-entropy + softmax is on the v0.4 roadmap. The trace is mathematically correct for the loss it implements.
